title: IACS Cyber Risk Assessment — Critical OT Asset Exposure

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for 4.2.3.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 4.2.3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.2: IACS Cyber Risk Assessment — Critical OT Asset Exposure.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.2' running on index=ot_assets, index=ot_vuln, optional ot_dmz_exposure.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk DB Connect (2686) for CMMS
spl:
index=ot_assets OR index=ot_vuln earliest=-7d
| eval asset_id=coalesce(device_id, asset_id, host)
| stats values(cve) as cves, max(cvss_base_score) as max_cvss, latest(criticality) as tier by asset_id
| lookup ot_dmz_exposure.csv asset_id OUTPUT exposed_to_untrusted
| where max_cvss>=7.0 OR exposed_to_untrusted="true"
| eval risk_note=case(isnotnull(cves) AND exposed_to_untrusted="true","Treat first: exposure + CVE", exposed_to_untrusted="true","Network exposure", true(),"Critical vulnerability concentration")
| table asset_id, tier, max_cvss, exposed_to_untrusted, risk_note, cves
description: Surfaces internet-facing or high-CVSS OT assets to inform documented risk treatment and residual risk acceptance for the IACS.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_assets, ot_vuln. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ingest vulnerability exports with OT asset keys; (2) refresh exposure lookup from Claroty/Nozomi topology; (3) align tiers with SL-T; (4) attach results to formal RA register. Indexes required: `index=ot_assets`, `index=ot_vuln`.
visualization: Scatter (CVSS vs count), Table (top assets), Single value (dual-risk count). Key fields: `asset_id`, `cves`, `exposed_to_untrusted`, `max_cvss`, `risk_note`, `tier`.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning. PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk DB Connect (2686) for CMMS.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_assets`, `index=ot_vuln`, optional `ot_dmz_exposure.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest vulnerability exports with OT asset keys; (2) refresh exposure lookup from Claroty/Nozomi topology; (3) align tiers with SL-T; (4) attach results to formal RA register.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_assets OR index=ot_vuln earliest=-7d
| eval asset_id=coalesce(device_id, asset_id, host)
| stats values(cve) as cves, max(cvss_base_score) as max_cvss, latest(criticality) as tier by asset_id
| lookup ot_dmz_exposure.csv asset_id OUTPUT exposed_to_untrusted
| where max_cvss>=7.0 OR exposed_to_untrusted="true"
| eval risk_note=case(isnotnull(cves) AND exposed_to_untrusted="true","Treat first: exposure + CVE", exposed_to_untrusted="true","Network exposure", true(),"Critical vulnerability concentration")
| table asset_id, tier, max_cvss, exposed_to_untrusted, risk_note, cves
```

Understanding this SPL

**IACS Cyber Risk Assessment — Critical OT Asset Exposure (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.2.3)** — Surfaces internet-facing or high-CVSS OT assets to inform documented risk treatment and residual risk acceptance for the IACS.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_assets`, `index=ot_vuln`, optional `ot_dmz_exposure.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk DB Connect (2686) for CMMS. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_assets, ot_vuln.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_assets, index=ot_vuln, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **asset_id** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **asset_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where max_cvss>=7.0 OR exposed_to_untrusted="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **risk_note** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
Output fields: asset_id, cves, exposed_to_untrusted, max_cvss, risk_note, tier.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Scatter (CVSS vs count), Table (top assets), Single value (dual-risk count).
grandmaExplanation: We surfaces internet-facing or high-CVSS OT assets to inform documented risk treatment and residual risk acceptance for the IACS. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Vulnerabilities data model (Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.severity | sort - count
